Bakassi Boys.description: Nigerian youth vigilante group active from 1998 to 2006, operating mainly in southeastern and south-southern Nigeria. They targeted armed robbers, ritual killers, and kidnappers, often using machetes and guns, and were accused of extrajudicial killings. The group claimed magical immunity to bullets and used a machete test to determine guilt. They later rebranded as Anambra Vigilante Services.

Colorado crime family.description: The Colorado crime family was an Italian-American Mafia crime family based in Pueblo and Denver, Colorado. It originated as a bootlegging organization under the Carlino brothers and later evolved into a La Cosa Nostra branch under Giuseppe Roma, known as the Smaldone crime family. The family engaged in racketeering, bootlegging, loansharking, extortion, drug trafficking, and illegal gambling. It is believed to have gone defunct in the 2000s after the death of Clarence Smaldone.

Wagner Group.description: Russian state-funded private military company controlled by Yevgeny Prigozhin until 2023, then by his son Pavel. Emerged during the war in Donbas in 2014, later played a major role in the Russian invasion of Ukraine, notably in the Battle of Bakhmut. Operates in Syria, Libya, Central African Republic, Mali, and other African countries, providing security in exchange for mining contracts. Accused of war crimes and led a brief armed rebellion against Russia in June 2023.

Clan Misso.description: Camorra clan operating in the Rione Sanità neighborhood of Naples, Italy. Founded in the 1980s by Giuseppe Misso, known as 'o nasone. Specializes in thefts, robberies of banks, post offices, and armored vans, as well as usury, counterfeiting, and gold trafficking. Engaged in violent feuds with rival clans, including a split in 2005 led by Salvatore Torino.

'Ndrina Barreca.description: The 'Ndrina Barreca is a Calabrian 'Ndrangheta clan based in Reggio Calabria, active in the southern area of the city since the 1980s. It split from the Ambrogio clan after the Pellaro feud in the late 1980s. The clan has been involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and violent conflicts, with several members becoming collaborators of justice. It has been targeted by multiple police operations, including Valanidi 2 and Barracuda.

'Ndrina Pesce.description: The Pesce 'ndrina is a powerful clan of the 'Ndrangheta based in Rosarno, Calabria, Italy. Founded in the 1950s, it engages in drug trafficking, extortion, and control of commercial businesses in the Gioia Tauro plain. The clan has been involved in port activities and public contracts, and its historical boss Giuseppe Pesce transformed it into an entrepreneurial organization. Currently led by Francesco Pesce after the arrests of Antonino and Salvatore Pesce, the clan remains active.

Clan Di Biasi.description: The Clan Di Biasi, also known as 'i Faiano', was a Camorra organization operating in the Quartieri Spagnoli of Naples, Italy. It was historically allied with the Giuliano clan but faced rivalry from the Russo clan. The group was involved in drug trafficking and other criminal activities. In 2008, a major police operation led to the arrest of several members, weakening the clan.

Strisciuglio clan.description: The Strisciuglio clan is a powerful criminal organization based in Bari, Italy, founded in the late 1990s by Domenico Strisciuglio. It operates primarily in the northern area of Bari and is involved in murder, drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, usury, and gambling. The clan has close ties with local politics and is considered the most powerful and bloodthirsty in the Bari area. Despite the imprisonment of its founder, the clan remains active under the leadership of his brother Sigismondo.

Penny Mobs.description: The Penny Mobs was a name used by the press for early street gangs in Glasgow, Scotland during the early 1870s. The name derived from the practice of gang members collecting a penny per head to pay fines as an alternative to imprisonment. The gangs formed following Irish immigration during the potato famines and declined after courts stopped offering fines for offenders.

'Ndrina Accorinti.description: The Accorinti are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Briatico, Tropea, and Zungri in the province of Vibo Valentia, Italy. Their existence was legally established in August 2020 following the 'Costa pulita' operation of 2016. They have ties with the Giampà 'ndrina of Lamezia Terme and the Mancuso clan. The clan is involved in drug trafficking, extortion, and other criminal activities.
